summary: Recent updates to the WebGPU backend in Firefox have significantly enhanced its functionality and stability. Notably, the **maximum buffer size for WebGPU has been increased to the safe limit**, allowing developers to create more complex and data-heavy applications without hitting performance constraints. Additionally, the introduction of stricter compliance flags ensures that WebGPU adheres more closely to the official specifications, improving compatibility and reliability. _These changes also include refined error handling, so the browser will not crash if a WebGPU device encounters a loss or internal error, providing a smoother user experience._ Overall, these improvements position Firefox to better support advanced graphics applications and gaming experiences on the web.
